commitments.expected_result: the agent classifies the ledger mode, checks current source surfaces, maps every external commitment to exactly one owner model, and blocks stale evidence
commitments.expected_terminal: the agent classifies the ledger mode, checks current source surfaces, maps every external commitment to exactly one owner model, and blocks stale evidence

commitments.label: broad behavior claims use a current behavior commitment ledger

relations.rationale: The development-process ledger governs the product-runtime UI promise without becoming its runtime owner.

commitments.expected_result: every in-scope candidate has exactly one supported visibility class; internal content never maps to ordinary UI; user-on-demand content is hidden before an explicit reveal and can return to the hidden state; actual visible content is compared with the allowed content set
commitments.expected_terminal: every in-scope candidate has exactly one supported visibility class; internal content never maps to ordinary UI; user-on-demand content is hidden before an explicit reveal and can return to the hidden state; actual visible content is compared with the allowed content set

commitments.trigger: the behavior ledger claims full source-to-commitment coverage
commitments.validation_boundary: owner model, contract-exhaustion cases, current TestMesh receipts, and project model checks

commitments.expected_result: missing, stale, progress-only, or release-only shard states block the claim
commitments.expected_terminal: every required TestMesh child has a current final passing receipt
commitments.failure_boundary: background liveness or stale receipts never count as final passing evidence
commitments.label: behavior ledger broad claims require current TestMesh shard evidence

relations.rationale: Current TestMesh receipts validate broad ledger claims.

commitments.expected_result: the miss is classified by plane, mapped to an existing same-plane commitment and owner model first, and backfilled only when no registered promise exists
commitments.expected_terminal: existing same-plane commitment is reused or one explicit coverage-gap candidate is registered
commitments.failure_boundary: ambiguous planes, guessed owners, or cross-plane false friends remain visible and cannot silently create instructions
commitments.label: model miss review backfeeds existing commitments before adding new behavior

relations.rationale: An AI model-miss operation may validate the related product-runtime UI promise while preserving separate ownership.

commitments.trigger: runtime, test, replay, log, or manual validation finds a miss after FlowGuard passed
commitments.validation_boundary: owner model, contract-exhaustion cases, current TestMesh receipts, and project model checks

commitments.expected_result: the declared official OpenSpec change is projected as current project-bounded read-only proposal, design, specification, task, and derived-status context
commitments.expected_terminal: one deterministic SpecContext is returned with no provider write, execution, session, cache, receipt, reconciliation, completion, or archive authority
commitments.failure_boundary: unsupported providers, unsafe change ids, out-of-root reads, missing authoring artifacts, mutable access, or any execution/evidence bridge remain visible and block context readiness
commitments.label: official OpenSpec remains independent while FlowGuard reads bounded authoring context
preconditions: one official OpenSpec change is explicitly selected under the current project root
commitments.trigger: a non-trivial task explicitly uses one official OpenSpec change as FlowGuard planning context
commitments.validation_boundary: official OpenSpec provider id, safe project-bounded change id, proposal/design/specification/task/status identities, deterministic hash, zero writes, and zero execution/evidence authority

commitments.expected_result: ordinary single-route work remains lightweight; when optimization is justified, only outcome-equivalent candidates are compared and the evidence-supported lower-rework route is selected within a named boundary
commitments.expected_terminal: one current process-optimization decision references TestMesh diagnostic evidence, relation-backed root-cause repair groups, ordinary primary owners, and current affected-obligation revalidation
commitments.failure_boundary: unnecessary optimizer ceremony, non-equivalent candidates, unaccounted diagnostic boundaries, unsafe parallelism, unrelated grouping, unowned repair, incomplete revalidation, stale decisions, and unsupported minimum/global claims remain visible and block use
commitments.label: DevelopmentProcessFlow conditionally chooses a lower-rework process without weakening outcome or evidence
preconditions: explicit_request, multiple_equivalent_routes, material_rework_risk, or diagnostic_boundary_choice is present
commitments.rationale: Reuse the DPF-owned internal strategy child as a small conditional process-quality decision, without a new route or ordinary-task ceremony

relations.rationale: Diagnostic completeness and execution evidence remain owned by TestMesh.

commitments.trigger: explicit_request, multiple_equivalent_routes, material_rework_risk, or diagnostic_boundary_choice justifies comparing process shapes
commitments.validation_boundary: inactive ordinary path, six hard equivalence dimensions, TestMesh diagnostic evidence, relation-backed repair groups, DPF freshness, ordinary Model-Test Alignment ownership, affected revalidation, and focused integration tests

commitments.expected_result: the old surface is deleted, blocked, migrated, delegated, repaired, or scoped out with evidence
commitments.expected_terminal: every old surface has one explicit non-success disposition or delegates to the singular primary path
commitments.failure_boundary: an old or alternate route cannot remain a second successful authority
commitments.label: old fields, aliases, wrappers, or alternate paths are disposed instead of kept as success paths
preconditions: an old path, alias, wrapper, field, or alternate success route is discovered

relations.rationale: Old-path disposition validates that the ledger has no alternate successful authority.

commitments.trigger: a FlowGuard change removes, replaces, or finds an old path or field
commitments.validation_boundary: owner model, contract-exhaustion cases, current TestMesh receipts, and project model checks

commitments.expected_result: the agent recalls same-plane commitments, selects the existing owner route, and loads detailed protocols only when needed
commitments.expected_terminal: one existing FlowGuard owner route is selected or one explicit no-match model boundary is recorded
commitments.failure_boundary: cross-plane keyword matches, duplicate routes, or missing current guidance evidence cannot silently become instructions
commitments.label: FlowGuard agent guidance delegates to one plane-first owner route

commitments.expected_result: the current portable model is validated and checked through one canonical interpreter, and every refinement or composition claim consumes explicit current mappings and contract tokens
commitments.expected_terminal: one canonical pass or one visible invalid, blocked, or failed report preserves the model identity and actionable counterexample
commitments.failure_boundary: non-current schemas, truncated exploration, unsafe or non-progressing graphs, unmapped child steps, strengthened assumptions, missing guarantees or providers, and conflicts cannot pass
commitments.label: portable finite models receive one fail-closed compositional verification result

path_authority.business_intent: validate and check a finite portable FlowGuard model or explicit composition/refinement claim

preconditions: the caller supplies one explicit current-schema finite artifact and any required explicit refinement binding
commitments.rationale: This is intrinsic FlowGuard product-runtime checking behavior, separate from agent guidance, release process, and any future application workflow.

commitments.validation_boundary: current schema/parser, reference interpreter, safety/temporal checks, explicit mappings, contract provider closure, native model, focused tests, and API/CLI parity

commitments.expected_result: the documented FlowGuard public names remain importable through one API_SURFACE-backed facade
commitments.expected_terminal: the public import resolves through the canonical API surface or fails visibly
commitments.failure_boundary: a second manual authority, missing export, or hidden alternate success path blocks compatibility confidence
commitments.label: FlowGuard public imports delegate to the canonical API surface

path_authority.business_intent: import the supported FlowGuard public API

commitments.rationale: This is product-runtime API behavior and is separate from AI guidance or development-process ownership.

commitments.validation_boundary: API surface registry, import facade, architecture models, and current API tests

commitments.expected_result: each supported template command delegates through the canonical template registry and preserves its documented output behavior
commitments.expected_terminal: the selected template is returned or written once, or a visible command error is returned
commitments.failure_boundary: duplicate wrapper authority, duplicate writes, or an automatic alternate success path blocks CLI confidence
commitments.label: FlowGuard template commands delegate to one registry dispatch path

path_authority.business_intent: execute one supported FlowGuard template command

commitments.rationale: This is product-runtime CLI behavior and remains distinct from agent-operation guidance.
commitments.trigger: a developer invokes a FlowGuard template command
commitments.validation_boundary: template registry, CLI facade, structure model, and current template tests
